info.name: RRA Tax Handbook Backend
info.description: Postman collection for the RRA Tax Handbook backend APIs, including auth, public content, admin content, users, roles, and supporting modules.

request.description: Login is username-only. The request field is named `username`, and the backend expects the auto-generated username: the first 3 letters of the first name plus the employee ID, normalized to lowercase alphanumeric text. Example: `Ndayishimiye` + `123456789` -> `nda123456789`.

Requests a password reset. For security, the response does not expose reset tokens by default. To receive resetToken and expiresAt in the response for local development, set APP_SECURITY_EXPOSE_SENSITIVE_TOKENS=true.
Completes a password reset using a valid token. The new password must be at least 8 characters and include uppercase, lowercase, number, and special character.

Returns the public invite-preview payload for a token, including email, fullName, autogenerated username, roleName, preferredLocale, expiresAt, and validity flags so the invited user can confirm which username to use when activating the account.
Returns a list of RoleResponse objects with fields id, name, description, and systemRole.
Deletes a custom role. Response shape is ApiResponse<String> with a top-level message and data confirmation string.
Updates a role using RoleRequest { name, description }. Response shape is ApiResponse<RoleResponse> with top-level message and data fields.
Creates a role using RoleRequest { name, description }. Response shape is ApiResponse<RoleResponse> with top-level message and data fields.
Updates the user's assigned role. Response shape is ApiResponse<UserResponse> with top-level message and data fields.
Updates the admin-managed account profile fields. Response shape is ApiResponse<UserResponse> with top-level message and data fields.
Returns a single RoleResponse with fields id, name, description, and systemRole.

Creates a local active user with the admin-managed account model. Password must be at least 8 characters and include uppercase, lowercase, number, and special character. Response shape is ApiResponse<UserResponse> with top-level message and data fields.

Creates a pending invited user using the admin-managed account model. inviteToken is hidden by default and is only returned in the response when APP_SECURITY_EXPOSE_SENSITIVE_TOKENS=true. Response shape is ApiResponse<UserInviteResponse> with data fields userId, email, optional inviteToken, optional expiresAt, and status.

Activates an invited account. Password must be at least 8 characters and include uppercase, lowercase, number, and special character. Response shape is ApiResponse<UserResponse> with top-level message and data fields.

Returns pending invited users for admin review. Each item includes the autogenerated username alongside employeeId, fullName, roleName, inviteToken, expiresAt, and status so admins can confirm the issued credential before the email is opened.
Reissues an invite token for a user in PENDING status. inviteToken is hidden by default and is only returned in the response when APP_SECURITY_EXPOSE_SENSITIVE_TOKENS=true.
Alias for resend-invite. inviteToken is hidden by default and is only returned in the response when APP_SECURITY_EXPOSE_SENSITIVE_TOKENS=true.
Restores a deactivated user by reissuing an invite. inviteToken is hidden by default and is only returned in the response when APP_SECURITY_EXPOSE_SENSITIVE_TOKENS=true.
Searches public articles using ArticleSearchRequest { keyword, language } and returns a list of ArticleResponse objects.
Returns the CMS-backed homepage payload with kicker, title, subtitle, searchLabel, helpLabel, updatedAt, and homepage cards for the requested locale.
Returns the CMS-backed homepage payload for the French locale.
Returns the CMS-backed homepage payload for the Kinyarwanda locale.
Returns public SectionSummaryResponse items with fields id, parentId, name, slug, summary, type, and sortOrder for the requested locale.
Returns a public TopicDetailResponse with fields id, sectionId, title, slug, summary, introText, topicType, status, scheduledPublishAt, and blocks.

request.description: Transitions a topic through editorial workflow. Supported actions now include SUBMIT_FOR_REVIEW, REQUEST_CHANGES, APPROVE, SCHEDULE_PUBLISH, PUBLISH, UNPUBLISH, and ARCHIVE. When using SCHEDULE_PUBLISH, include a future ISO-8601 scheduledAt timestamp. Response shape is ApiResponse<TopicWorkflowResponse> with data fields topicId, title, slug, status, action, performedBy, and optional scheduledPublishAt.

Publishes any approved topics whose scheduledPublishAt is due. This is the current MVP release mechanism until an automated scheduler is introduced. Response shape is ApiResponse<ScheduledPublishProcessingResponse> with data.processedCount.
Returns audit log entries directly as AuditLog objects with fields id, action, actor, targetEmail, details, and createdAt.
